title: Boo York, Boo York! Trump gets wake-up call in city that never sleeps
content: Donald Trump's appearance at an NBA Finals game in New York City was met with widespread boos and jeers, overshadowing the Knicks' loss and causing significant traffic disruptions. Despite the negative reception, Trump claimed the reaction was mostly cheers, while New Yorkers expressed their displeasure with the presidential visit.

title: Ben Stokes likely to 'retire' from Test cricket amid nightclub controversy
content: “I fear from everything I hear that Stokes is going to act first. And, regretfully, I hear that he is going to step down and possibly even retire."

title: TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee skips Bengal CID’s third notice in signature ‘forgery’ case
content: Diamond Harbour MP Abhishek Banerjee has once again skipped the state CID's summons in a signature forgery case, citing prior engagements in Delhi. This marks the third instance of the Trinamool leader avoiding the agency's questioning. A CID team had previously visited his residence to serve the latest notice.

title: Apple's AI boss Craig Federighi has a message for OpenAI, Anthropic, and other AI rivals
content: At WWDC 2026, Apple's Craig Federighi called out OpenAI, Anthropic, and other AI rivals for treating privacy as an afterthought—arguing most force users to actively opt out of data retention. Apple's counter is Private Cloud Compute, now expanded to Google Cloud for the first time, with verifiable, auditable guarantees that user data is never stored or accessed. It's Apple's most aggressive privacy-first AI argument yet.

title: India's nuclear weapons spending rose in 2025: How does it compare with China and Pakistan?
content: Global spending on nuclear weapons hit an all-time high of nearly $119 billion in 2025, a 19% increase, fueling concerns of a new arms race. China is identified as the fastest-growing nuclear power, while major nations are modernizing arsenals and increasing deployable warheads despite declining stockpiles.

title: 9 injured after balcony ledge collapses near Banke Bihari Temple in Mathura
content: A portion of a ledge wall collapsed near the Banke Bihari Temple in Mathura. Several devotees sustained injuries in the incident. Local residents and emergency services provided immediate assistance. The injured were taken to nearby hospitals for treatment. Police have cordoned off the area and are investigating the cause of the collapse.

title: Watch: TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee heckled with 'Chor Chor' chants at Kolkata airport; loses cool
content: TMC MP Kalyan Banerjee was heckled at Kolkata airport amidst internal party turmoil. He accused BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari of being a thief. The party faces a potential crisis with reports of at least 20 MPs supporting the NDA, threatening its state influence and parliamentary position.

title: Layoffs announced at Sam Altman's $2.5 billion startup amid revenue struggles
content: Sam Altman's eyeball-scanning startup, Tools for Humanity, is reportedly cutting jobs as it navigates its next strategic phase. Despite a significant valuation and user sign-ups, the company faces challenges in demonstrating revenue generation and regulatory approval for its "Orb" technology. This news emerges as OpenAI, also led by Altman, files for its IPO.

title: 'Modi's loot model': Rahul Gandhi slams Centre over LPG price hike, Ujjwala subsidy cuts
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized the Centre for rising LPG prices and reduced Ujjwala subsidies, alleging that government policies are burdening poor and middle-class families. He claimed millions are forced back to firewood due to "anti-poor economic policies" and a "compromised foreign policy."

title: Hugh Laurie proved he actually was House MD, till he apologised
content: Hugh Laurie, the actor behind Dr. House, defended the show's repetitive formula against a critic, likening it to artistic variations. He argued the series explored deeper moral philosophy beyond medical diagnoses. Laurie's witty, House-like response was followed by a surprisingly decent apology, highlighting the actor's humanity beyond his iconic character.

title: TCS chairman N Chandrasekaran says company has no layoff plans
content: TCS Chairman N Chandrasekaran confirmed no layoffs and a continued hiring strategy, though massive campus hiring may cease. He emphasized AI as a significant opportunity, not a threat, with AI revenues nearing $2.5 billion annually and projected to be 100% of revenue by 2028-2030.  AI is seen as intelligence infrastructure, creating new opportunities and talent needs.

title: Penguins Surface in Trade Buzz Around Red Wings' $8.7M Forward
content: New NHL trade speculation links the Pittsburgh Penguins to Detroit Red Wings captain Dylan Larkin. There’s no reported deal, but analysts think Pittsburgh has the assets and salary cap flexibility to explore a move. The speculation comes as the Penguins seek offensive reinforcements and the Red Wings have questions about their long-term direction.

title: Celina Jaitly visits Mahakaleshwar temple amid ongoing divorce battle with Peter Haag
content: Celina Jaitly recently visited the sacred Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga in Ujjain and shared a heartfelt note about her spiritual experience. The visit comes at a challenging time in her personal life, as she remains embroiled in an ongoing legal and marital dispute with her estranged husband, Peter Haag. Taking to Instagram, Celina posted a series of pictures from the temple and reflected on the profound impact the visit had on her.

title: Setback for Congress: Meenakshi Natarajan's Rajya Sabha nomination rejected
content: Meenakshi Natarajan, a Congress Rajya Sabha candidate from Madhya Pradesh, had her nomination rejected due to alleged concealment of a pending criminal case in Telangana from her affidavit. BJP candidate Mahesh Kewat filed the complaint, citing Supreme Court guidelines. Congress countered, stating a show-cause notice doesn't require affidavit mention and accusing the BJP of harassment.

title: Simone Biles & Jonathan Owens' 2026 net worth and luxury lifestyle
content: Simone Biles and Jonathan Owens have an estimated combined net worth of $27 million in 2026. Simone accounts for about $25 million through endorsements, appearances, tours, and investments, while Jonathan’s $2 million comes mainly from NFL contracts. The couple also owns valuable real estate and enjoys a luxury lifestyle, helping secure their financial future beyond sports.

title: Patriots linked to $31M giants pass rusher in surprise trade rumor
content: The New York Giants’ star player Kayvon Thibodeaux has been a constant name in the trade market for the last few weeks as there have been a number of rumors about him even as he continues to remain in the New York Giants. Amid all the mess, the New England Patriots have now emerged as a potential landing spot for Kayvon Thibodeaux.

title: Oilers-Sebastian Cossa trade rumors grow despite no red wings deal
content: Social media has been buzzing with rumours linking Sebastian Cossa to the Edmonton Oilers, but there's no proof of a done deal with the Detroit Red Wings. Edmonton goaltending has been a topic of speculation, but Sebastian Cossa remains in the Red Wings fold, and nothing official has come down on a trade.

title: ATF price stabilisation plan: Jet fuel prices rise 10% as oil retailers roll out scheme
content: Aviation turbine fuel prices rose by 10% as a government-backed price stabilization scheme locks in rates for airlines at Rs 115 per litre for up to three years. This voluntary scheme aims to shield carriers from global oil price volatility, with the government providing interest-free advances to oil companies if benchmark prices exceed Rs 86.32 per litre.

title: China check: New US base in Australia signals AUKUS push, to host nuclear submarines
content: Australia's new Naval Support Activity Stirling bolsters the AUKUS pact, facilitating US and UK nuclear submarine rotations. This strategic base near key maritime routes aims to enhance Indo-Pacific security amidst regional assertiveness. Significant Australian investment is fueling this expansion, including acquiring advanced Virginia-class submarines.

title: IND A vs SL A: Prabhsimran's costly blunder sparks controversy; Gaikwad furious
content: India A players were left fuming after a controversial run-out decision in their Tri-Nation A Series clash against Sri Lanka A. Wicketkeeper Prabhsimran Singh dislodged the bails before the ball hit the stumps, leading to Chamika Karunaratne being given not out despite appearing short of his crease.

title: Pralay: The missile that lets India strike deep — without going nuclear
content: Ballistic missiles are evolving beyond strategic roles, now carrying conventional warheads to strike deep targets. Iran and Russia exemplify this shift. India's Pralay, a short-range tactical missile, offers a conventionally armed option, capable of hitting key enemy infrastructure up to 500 km away and evading defenses, complementing other Indian weapon systems.

title: At least 7 burnt alive, several injured after blast at illegal fireworks godown in Jaipur
content: A devastating blast and subsequent fire at an alleged illegal fireworks godown in Jaipur's Khoh Nagoriyan area claimed at least seven lives on Tuesday morning. Several others sustained injuries in the incident. Authorities are investigating the cause, with safety lapses and illegal storage being considered.

title: India deploys 12 nuclear warheads for first time; where do China and Russia stand?
content: India has deployed 12 nuclear warheads for the first time, a significant shift in its nuclear posture, according to SIPRI. With a total of 190 warheads, this move signals a move towards a more operationally ready sea-based deterrent. This development occurs as global nuclear arsenals expand, with China rapidly increasing its stockpile.

title: Who is Totoa Auvaa? 125kg rugby player tried to hit Stokes' teammate in nightclub row
content: The England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) confirmed on Monday that the pair were present at a nightclub in the early hours after the match and that an incident took place.

title: Cardio or strength training: Harvard study finally picks one for longevity
content: A landmark Harvard study reveals strength training, not just cardio, is crucial for longevity. Aiming for 90-120 minutes weekly significantly slashes death risks from all causes, heart disease, and brain conditions. Combining strength with cardio offers the greatest health boost, proving muscle is vital for healthy aging and independence, not just aesthetics.

title: Suvendu Adhikari’s first month as CM: The Nayak phase of power
content: West Bengal's new BJP government is actively implementing changes. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari is focusing on re-imagining the state with swift policy rollouts. Key initiatives target women and youth, while nationalistic themes are prominent. The Trinamool Congress faces a significant crisis. The government is also enforcing laws and promoting central schemes.

title: SpaceX IPO: Musk's firm sees huge oversubscription as institutional orders top $10 billion
content: SpaceX's IPO has garnered over $10 billion in institutional orders, signaling immense demand for what could be the largest public offering in history. The company is set to price its shares at $135, aiming to raise $75 billion and achieve a $1.8 trillion valuation. Retail investors can still participate in the heavily oversubscribed offering.

title: Russian crude here to stay? Why India-US energy ties may be more about LPG, LNG than oil
content: Geopolitical developments in the last few months have dictated India’s energy supplies. Be it crude oil, LPG or LNG, India is largely dependent on the world to meet its needs, and the US-Iran war has disrupted supplies via the Strait of Hormuz.

title: ‘Toxic’: Production team rubbishes rumours of 100 day reshoot for Yash and Geetu Mohandas’ film
content: Rumors of a 100-day reshoot for Yash's 'Toxic' have been officially debunked by the production team. Despite multiple release date shifts, the film's progress remains on track as planned. Fans eagerly await the movie, Yash's first since 'KGF: Chapter 2', directed by Geetu Mohandas.

title: From Parliament to premieres: Kangana Ranaut's saree love is changing fashion conversations
content: Kangana Ranaut is revitalizing the saree's relevance by consistently wearing Indian weaves for everyday occasions, from Parliament to film promotions. Her practical styling and embrace of handloom fabrics demonstrate that traditional attire can be both modern and professional, challenging the notion that sarees are solely for special events.

title: Arjun Rampal recalls darkest phase amid separation and mother’s cancer battle
content: Arjun Rampal candidly shared the profound emotional turmoil following his divorce and the loss of his mother to cancer, describing it as a dark, lonely period. He found solace and strength in his relationship with Gabriella Demetriades, with whom he weathered the storm and built a new life, now parents to two sons.

title: Hero MotoCorp buyer gets Rs 88,000 refund over recurring engine issue
content: A Bengaluru consumer court ordered Hero MotoCorp and its dealers to refund Rs 88,000 for a motorcycle with a recurring engine defect. Despite over 10 service visits and numerous part replacements, the bike's engine repeatedly shut off while riding. The court found a manufacturing defect, citing the company's own admission of the issue during test rides.

title: TN CM Vijay launches Special Force, says drones will be used to prevent crimes against women
content: The chief minister linked crimes against women with the growing menace of drug trafficking. He said the govt would take strong measures to eradicate the drug network and improve law and order.

title: Tragic end for Aggarwal family: After losing 8 relatives in Delhi fire, 75-year-old dies at hospital
content: A devastating Delhi hotel fire tragically claimed the lives of eight members of the Aggarwal family, including a son, daughter-in-law, and grandchildren. The sole survivor, 75-year-old Radhe Shyam Aggarwal, has since passed away. The blaze, which killed 22 in total, exposed severe safety lapses at the Flourish Inn, including illegal construction and a lack of fire systems.

title: NEET paper leak accused seeks interim bail to appear for re-exam
content: A Delhi court is considering an interim bail plea for NEET-UG paper leak accused Yash Yadav, who seeks to attend the re-examination. The court has allowed him study materials and will hear the CBI's response on June 12. The investigation has revealed a network distributing leaked papers for profit across multiple states.

title: Iran deal 'very close'? 37 times Trump's predictions fell flat
content: President Trump repeatedly declared a deal with Iran imminent for months, even after a ceasefire was announced. Despite his optimistic pronouncements, tensions persist as Iran launched retaliatory attacks following Israeli strikes, straining a fragile ceasefire and raising questions about US influence.

title: Bills eye former saints all-pro in potential offensive upgrade
content: The Buffalo Bills have been linked to a potential trade for New Orleans Saints running back Alvin Kamara after FOX Sports insider Greg Auman named Buffalo as a possible landing spot. Kamara, now 30, is coming off a down season and carries an $11.5 million salary for 2026. While the veteran still offers value as a pass-catching back, many around the league question whether adding him would truly address Buffalo’s need for more explosive offensive playmakers.

title: Watch: Judge Strikes Down Trump’s H-1B Fee: Implications for India and the U.S. | Above the Fold | 09.06.2026
content: From a major U.S. court setback to Donald Trump’s H-1B visa fee plan to escalating political turmoil in West Bengal and fresh developments in the CMRL case involving T Veena - Top stories of the day

title: Stand-off over Bengaluru Development portfolio likely to be discussed in Delhi
content: Shivakumar will be in Delhi on Wednesday to meet senior party leaders including Sonia Gandhi and Rahul Gandhi. Krishna Byre Gowda, who is yet to take charge of the portfolio, is already in Delhi

title: Delay in final seat matrix pushes CET counselling dates
content: In absence of a Higher Education Minister, the final seat matrix and fee for engineering courses has to be approved by the Chief Minister

title: Mamata and Sonia hold talks on strategy, discuss alliance for future polls
content: The two leaders had a closed door meeting at Ms. Gandhi’s residence in Delhi, a day after the INDIA bloc meeting

title: Dalit youth allegedly beaten to death over relationship with upper-caste girl in Uttarakhand
content: The FIR states that the victim told his father that he and his friend had been called to the accused person’s house around midnight and were allegedly assaulted through the night

title: Rajya Sabha polls: Congress candidate from Madhya Pradesh Meenakshi Natarajan’s nomination rejected
content: Congress leader K.C. Venugopal led a delegation meet the ECI to represent Meenakshi Natarajan’s case; the delegation was not allowed inside and the leaders sat on a protest outside the ECI

title: Long wait finally ends for loyalist with BJP choosing Lingaraj Patil for Council polls
content: Going by the numbers, Lingaraj Patil is likely to get elected to the Legislative Council without any hassles

title: Naidu orders monthly economic reports to track GSDP growth
content: Chief Minister calls for AI-based monitoring of economic activity, improved revenue collection and performance evaluation based on actual growth outcomes

title: Governor “stays” decisions of DUK BoG Meeting
content: The move comes on the basis of allegations that the BoG meeting was convened by its chairperson whose term had allegedly expired

title: CM playing up Z+ security and bungalow row to divert attention from real issues: Tejashwi Yadav
content: The RJD leader says withdrawal of Z+ security for Lalu Prasad and Rabri Devi and the notice to Devi to vacate the bungalow at 10, Circular Road, Patna, are not the real issues. Picking up from Nishant Kumar’s election affidavit, RJD says he is not an engineer as popularly believed.

title: West Bengal CID searches Mamata Banerjee’s residence in ‘forged signatures’ probe
content: After a standoff with party supporters, a team of half a dozen CID officials entered the premises, saying they wished to search the party office on the premises, not the former CM’s residence

title: RTIH invites applications for Rural Entrepreneurship Fellows programme
content: Apart from a monthly stipend of ₹10,000, the selected fellows will gain practical exposure to grassroots entrepreneurship initiatives, mentoring from experts and participate in innovation programmes

title: Centre announces interim allocation for VB-GRAMG
content: Allocation made ahead of rules to ensure seamless shift from MGNREGS, says Rural Development Minister; says no State will see a fund cut; Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu get highest allocations

title: State urged to end a decade of neglect towards tourism sector in Kerala
content: A delegation led by the CKTI has demanded that at least 1% of the total revenue generated by tourism be reinvested in the sector for development, marketing and infrastructure

title: World Leaders Praise PM Modi As He Becomes India's Longest-Serving Prime Minister
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi etched his name into India's political history books on Tuesday, surpassing Jawaharlal Nehru's record to become the country's longest-serving elected Prime Minister.

title: Akali Dal Rebel Joins Waris Punjab De, Boosts Party's Statewide Ambitions
content: With Dakha MLA Manpreet Singh Ayali's entry, the Amritpal Singh-led party has gained a credible foothold in the crucial Malwa belt.

title: Rajya Sabha Pick Meenakshi Natarajan's Nomination Junked, Congress Protests
content: The MLAs, who have been waiting to take a flight to Bengaluru, meanwhile, were called back when Natarajan's nomination was rejected. Sources said the flight was recalled from the runway.

title: Delhi Aims To Clear Drains By June 15 To Curb Waterlogging This Monsoon
content: The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) on Tuesday said nearly 70 per cent of desilting work in stormwater drains under its jurisdiction has been completed.

title: India Adds More Nuclear Warheads: Where Do Pakistan, China Stand?
content: Russia and the United States continue to dominate the global nuclear order by a wide margin. Russia holds an estimated 4,400 nuclear warheads, followed by the US with 3,700.

title: Punjab On Path To Becoming Startup Hub Of India: Bhagwant Mann
content: Describing startups as a key pillar of Punjab's future economic growth, Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann on Tuesday said his government is committed to ensuring that no promising idea is abandoned due to a lack of financial support.

title: 14 Kuki Hostages Released By Naga Groups In Manipur After 27 Days
content: The release came after central and state governments ensured that all possible efforts would be made to trace and establish the status of the six missing Naga hostages.

title: Watch: Gorilla's Human-Like Reaction After Fight With Mate Wins Internet
content: According to reports, the footage was captured shortly after Kiyomasa had a spat with his mate, a detail that only added fuel to the internet's imagination.

title: US Iran War Live: Crew Rescued After US Helicopter Goes Down Near Hormuz
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran closed the airspace around Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country's main airfield, after the Israeli attack.

title: Fake IDs, Fake Cop Badges, Fake Money: Bengal Killer Couple's 'World Of Fake'
content: Police found multiple fake identity cards, counterfeit badges resembling those of the West Bengal Police, and a fake badge identifying the holder as a railway ticket checker.

title: After Japan, Nepal Bans Import Of Indian Mangoes Over This Issue
content: Before Nepal, Japan last month suspended mango imports from India after its quarantine officials found lapses in pest-control procedures at Indian treatment facilities during inspections earlier this year.

title: Design Drew Big Tech Firms To India: Minister On Semi-Conductor Prospects
content: Complex chips for commercial purposes will be manufactured at two facilities in India. A third will begin production in July, and a fourth by December, Ashwini Vaishnaw said.

title: Protests, Poverty And Power: The Story Of Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir
content: To understand PoK, one must go back to 1947. When British India was partitioned, the Indian Independence Act gave princely states three choices: join India, join Pakistan, or remain independent.

title: Man Strangles Lover's Husband, Leaves Body Under Bike, Tells Her 'Program Ok'
content: The victim, Kiran, allegedly used to harass his wife and that is said to have been a contributing factor in the murder plan.

title: India's Nuclear Arsenal Swells To 190: Which Country Has Most Nuke Warheads
content: For the first time, India has kept 12 nuclear warheads deployed during peacetime, breaking with its longstanding practice of storing warheads separately from delivery systems such as ballistic missiles, according to SIPRI.

title: Rs 1.23 Lakh Crore In 78 Days: Sources On Support To Oil Firms After Iran War
content: After the first 78 days, the Ministry of Finance determined that extending further financial support to a single sector would not be appropriate, the sources said.

title: No Trump-Putin Call Planned, No Timeline On US Envoys' Visit: Russia
content: American negotiators Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner continue to maintain contacts with both Russia and Ukraine, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said.

title: 8 Relatives Died In Delhi Fire, Now Patriarch Dies, Family Wiped Out
content: Radhe Shyam Aggarwal had been inquiring about the absence of his family members at the hospital, left completely unaware of what had happened just a few hundred metres away from the hospital.

title: US Sanctions Alibaba, BYD, Baidu In Crackdown On Chinese Companies
content: The list, updated and published Monday by the Pentagon, now sanctions well-known, non-state-owned Chinese companies that are not traditionally considered to be in the defence or security sector.

title: 'PoK Is Jallianwala Bagh, Asim Munir Is General Dwyer': Ex J&K Top Cop
content: Likening the situation in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir (PoK) to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre, former Jammu and Kashmir Director General of Police SP Vaid accused Field Marshal General Asim Munir of acting like General Dyer.

title: University faculty recruitment application deadline extended till June 15 in A.P.
content: The last date for receipt of hard copies of applications, along with supporting documents, by registered post, speed post or courier has also been extended from June 15 to June 22

title: TTD takes possession of land for Sri Venkateswara temple in Assam
content: Assam government hands over 10-acre plot at Amerigog village to TTD; foundation stone for the first Sri Venkateswara temple in the Northeast to be laid soon

title: Rahul Gandhi questions surge in Adani's fortunes under Modi govt
content: The Adani Group is in the eye of a storm following the allegations of fraud and stock manipulation by a U.S.-based short-selling firm

title: Have sought time for Rahul Gandhi to speak in Lok Sabha on March 21: Kharge
content: Rahul Gandhi's speech was made in Jammu and Kashmir and the police are asking about it here, the Congress chief said

title: Congress’ Shaktisinh Gohil files breach of privilege notice against Piyush Goyal
content: Mr. Goyal said in the Parliament he had raised a serious matter regarding a senior Congress leader going abroad and stating that "democracy is in danger" in India and even sought the U.S. and Europe to interfere in the country's affairs

title: CBSE schools in Bengaluru reschedule timetable to accommodate teachers on revaluation duty
content: Some plan to conduct extra classes to make up for lost time and cover the syllabus for the upcoming midterm exams in September

title: Rahul Gandhi asked to vacate official residence following disqualification as Member of Parliament
content: A letter was sent to the Congress leader by the Lok Sabha Housing Committee asking him to vacate his 12 Tughlaq Lane bungalow by April 22
